- `AbilityCooldownGroup` (interface): interface AbilityCooldownGroup ‚Äî A cooldown shared across every member slot: casting any member starts it, and no member can cast while it runs. One group that every slot joins is an MMO global cooldown.
- `AbilityKit` (interface): interface AbilityKit ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `AbilityKitOptions` (interface): interface AbilityKitOptions ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `AbilityResource` (interface): interface AbilityResource ‚Äî A spendable resource pool (mana, energy, rage) bound to an ability kit. When bound, `cast` deducts a slot's `resourceCost` on success and `state`/`snapshot`/`canCast` reflect affordability without a per-call `resourceAvailable` argument ‚Äî the cast path actually spends instead of only gating (#357).

- `AbilitySlotRetune` (interface): interface AbilitySlotRetune ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented

## @jgengine/core/combat/castRunner

- `CastBarSnapshot` (interface): interface CastBarSnapshot ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `CastConfig` (interface): interface CastConfig ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented

- `CastInterruptReason` (type): type CastInterruptReason = "moved" | "cancelled" | "replaced" ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `CastRunner` (interface): interface CastRunner ‚Äî Per-entity cast-time state machine ‚Äî begin, tick with game-time `dt` plus how far the caster moved, and act on the returned event. The runner owns timing and move-interruption only; the caller spends resources, starts cooldowns, and executes the ability when `completed` fires (compose with `abilityKit` ‚Äî check readiness before `begin`, `cast` on completion).
- `createCastRunner` (function): function createCastRunner(): CastRunner ‚Äî Run a channeled cast/charge timer that movement or damage can interrupt ‚Äî the spell cast bar.

## @jgengine/core/combat/dotField

- `DotField` (interface): interface DotField ‚Äî A pool of timed damage-over-time instances keyed by string (`bleed`, `poison`, `burn`) ‚Äî the recurring-damage counterpart to `breakMeters`' one-shot buildup procs (#536.4). `tick` advances every active DoT and returns the damage due this frame, one entry per interval crossed, so the game routes each through its own effect pipeline; a pure scheduler that never applies damage itself.
- `DotSpec` (interface): interface DotSpec ‚Äî A single damage-over-time instance: how hard, how often, and for how long.
- `DotTick` (interface): interface DotTick ‚Äî One damage event (or expiry marker) emitted by `DotField.tick` for the game to route through its effects.
- `createDotField` (function): function createDotField(): DotField ‚Äî Builds an empty {@link DotField}; `apply` DoTs onto it and drain damage each frame with `tick`.

## @jgengine/core/combat/magazine

- `Magazine` (interface): interface Magazine ‚Äî A per-weapon magazine: discrete loaded rounds, a timed reload that refills from a reserve pool, and the reserve-pool interaction itself ‚Äî the primitive that replaces hand-rolling mag size, reload delay, and reserve bookkeeping per game (#536.2).
- `MagazineConfig` (interface): interface MagazineConfig ‚Äî Tuning for `createMagazine`: mag capacity, reload delay, and where reserve ammo is drawn from.
- `MagazineReserve` (interface): interface MagazineReserve ‚Äî Draws ammo for a `Magazine`'s reload from wherever the reserve pool actually lives.
- `createMagazine` (function): function createMagazine(config: MagazineConfig): Magazine ‚Äî Builds a {@link Magazine}: discrete loaded ammo, a timed reload, and reserve-pool interaction.

## @jgengine/core/combat/regenShield

- `RegenShield` (interface): interface RegenShield ‚Äî A shield pool that stops regenerating for `regenDelayMs` after every hit, then refills at `regenPerSecond` ‚Äî the delayed-regen primitive that replaces snapshot-comparing stat values per tick to detect "damage taken" (#536.3). `damage` resets the grace timer; `tick` counts it down and regenerates once it elapses.
- `RegenShieldConfig` (interface): interface RegenShieldConfig ‚Äî Tuning for `createRegenShield`: pool size, refill rate, and the post-damage grace period.
- `createRegenShield` (function): function createRegenShield(config: RegenShieldConfig): RegenShield ‚Äî Builds a {@link RegenShield} that suppresses regen for `regenDelayMs` after each hit.

## @jgengine/core/combat/shotOrigin

- `DEFAULT_EYE_HEIGHT` (const): const DEFAULT_EYE_HEIGHT: number ‚Äî Shot-origin and first-person camera eye height above an entity's position: 90% of the default 1.8m hitbox top.
- `ResolvedShot` (interface): interface ResolvedShot ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `ShotOriginDeps` (interface): interface ShotOriginDeps ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `ShotOriginPolicy` (type): type ShotOriginPolicy = | { kind: "converge"; muzzle?: EntityPosition; height?: number } | { kind: "eye"; height?: number } | { kind: "legacy" } | { kind: "entity" } | { kind: "entityOffset"; offset: EntityPosition } | { kind: "muzzle"; offset?: EntityPosition } | { kind: "camera"; origin: EntityPos‚Ä¶ ‚Äî How a shot's world-space origin (and optional direction) is resolved before prediction/settlement. - `converge` ‚Äî the shot leaves the gun `muzzle` yet still passes through whatever the shooter's sightline (crosshair) covers: origin is the muzzle offset, direction is bent from the muzzle to the eye ray's aim point. The projectile system's default for a free `{ yaw, pitch }` aim, so a bullet visibly comes from the barrel without missing the reticle. Needs a scene raycast to find the aim point (`convergeShot`); a bare `resolveShot` degrades to a straight muzzle ray. Passes an explicit `{ origin, direction }` aim through untouched. - `eye` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's entity position raised to eye height; the shot traces the shooter's sightline, so what the crosshair covers is what gets hit. - `legacy` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's raw entity position (feet). - `entity` ‚Äî always the shooter's entity position. - `entityOffset` / `muzzle` ‚Äî entity-local offset rotated by the shooter's yaw (muzzle on a weapon model). - `camera` ‚Äî explicit camera/reticle world origin (and optional direction override). - `world` ‚Äî absolute world origin.
- `aimDirection` (function): function aimDirection(aim: Aim): EntityPosition | null ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `aimSpreadDeg` (function): function aimSpreadDeg(aim: Aim): number ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `convergeShot` (function): function convergeShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, range: number, sightHit: (origin: EntityPosition, direction: EntityPosition) => EntityPosition | null, muzzleOffset?: EntityPosition): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî Resolves a `converge` shot with scene knowledge: fires from the gun muzzle but bends the direction so the shot passes through the aim point the shooter's eye ray covers. `sightHit` casts the eye ray and returns where it lands (first impact), or `null` to fall back to a point `range` metres down the sightline. A `{ origin, direction }` aim is passed through unchanged (nothing to converge).
- `eyeHeightFromColliders` (function): function eyeHeightFromColliders(set: EntityColliderSet | null | undefined): number ‚Äî Eye height derived from a collider set: 90% of the tallest hitbox top, or the humanoid default when unknown.
- `resolveShot` (function): function resolveShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, policy: ShotOriginPolicy = { kind: "eye" }): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented

- `createStats` (function): function createStats<TStat extends string>(base: Record<TStat, number>, options?: CreateStatsOptions): Stats<TStat> ‚Äî A stat block whose base values take stacking, timed buffs and debuffs, resolving the modified value on read.

## @jgengine/core/tactics/fallingGrid

- `FallingGrid` (interface): interface FallingGrid<TCell> ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `FallingGridCell` (type): type FallingGridCell = readonly [number, number] ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `FallingGridConfig` (interface): interface FallingGridConfig ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `FallingGridSnapshot` (interface): interface FallingGridSnapshot<TCell> ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `GravityIntervalConfig` (interface): interface GravityIntervalConfig ‚Äî ‚ö† undocumented
- `LockState` (interface): interface LockState ‚Äî Elapsed grounded time for the lock-delay state machine. `null` means airborne / not timing.
- `createFallingGrid` (function): function createFallingGrid<TCell>(config: FallingGridConfig): FallingGrid<TCell> ‚Äî A game-agnostic cell grid for gravity-driven falling-block mechanics: bounded occupancy, footprint placement/collision, line-clear collapse, column-gravity settle (for match-3/sand style cascades), and a pure lock-delay helper. Extracted from falling-block tetris logic so any falling-block game can adopt it instead of reimplementing the grid.
- `gravityIntervalMs` (function): function gravityIntervalMs(level: number, config: GravityIntervalConfig = {}): number ‚Äî Gravity tick interval for a given level: linear speed-up with a floor, generalizing a falling-block `gravityInterval` (`max(0.05, 0.8 - level * 0.07)` seconds) to milliseconds with configurable base/step/floor.
